name: Merrill Lynch Financial Advisor Gregg Fraistat
description_short: The Lipski Group is a Merrill Lynch Wealth Management team in Midtown Manhattan offering comprehensive financial advisory services to business owners, executives, and professionals.
description_long:
The Lipski Group operates as part of Merrill Lynch Wealth Management, based in New York City at 114 West 47th Street in Midtown Manhattan. The firm was founded to deliver sophisticated financial advice and superior execution to a clientele of business owners, financial professionals, attorneys, and senior executives at both public and private companies. Gregg Fraistat serves as a First Vice President and Senior Financial Advisor within this team.

The Lipski Group provides comprehensive wealth management services including personal banking, lending, investment solutions, retirement and savings account planning, insurance, and wealth planning. They offer access to a wide range of investment solutions from Merrill Lynch and banking products from Bank of America. Their approach centers on identifying and prioritizing client goals rather than focusing on asset minimums or transaction volumes. Services include strategic planning, life insurance, corporate benefits, equity compensation planning, and guidance on business transitions.

The team distinguishes itself through specialized expertise in complex financial planning for high-net-worth individuals and business owners. All three lead advisors—Adam Lipski, Gregg Fraistat, and Colby Tragni—hold Forbes recognition as "Best-in-State Wealth Management Teams" (2023-2026). Adam Lipski additionally earned Forbes "Best-in-State Wealth Advisors" (2024-2026) and "Top Next-Gen Wealth Advisors" (2024) recognition. The group emphasizes tailored solutions and deep understanding of client needs.

Important caveat: This is a traditional wealth management firm, not a credit repair service. The current categorization as "credit-repair" is incorrect. The Lipski Group serves affluent clients seeking comprehensive wealth planning, investment management, and insurance solutions—not consumers seeking credit score improvement or dispute services. Prospective clients should expect high minimum asset requirements typical of Merrill Lynch Wealth Management and should be prepared to work with advisors specializing in business owner and executive financial planning.
address: 1 Bryant Pk 3rd Floor, New York, NY 10036

pros: Team members recognized as Forbes "Best-in-State Wealth Management Teams" (2023-2026) and Best Wealth Advisors
Specialized expertise in equity compensation, corporate benefits, and business transitions for executives
Access to both Merrill Lynch investment solutions and Bank of America banking products through integrated platform
Customized approach focusing on client goals rather than product placement
Located in Midtown Manhattan with dedicated contact number (212) 382-8526 and email access
Deep experience serving business owners, financial professionals, attorneys, and C-suite executives
Emphasis on long-term wealth planning and multi-generational wealth transfer strategies
cons: Not appropriate for consumers seeking credit repair or credit score improvement—this is a wealth management firm for high-net-worth clients
Likely carries minimum asset requirements typical of Merrill Lynch Wealth Management practices (not disclosed on website)
Limited information on fee structure, advisory services model, or minimum account sizes on public website
Primarily focused on business owners and executives, not accessible or relevant for average consumer credit issues
No indication of specific pricing transparency or fiduciary commitment clarity on public-facing materials
best_for: Business owners and entrepreneurs planning for equity compensation and business transitions
Executives and senior management seeking comprehensive wealth planning and tax optimization
High-net-worth individuals and families focused on multi-generational wealth transfer and legacy planning
Financial professionals and attorneys requiring specialized advisory services integrated with banking products
diagnosis: The Lipski Group is a wealth management firm designed for affluent business owners, executives, and high-net-worth individuals—not a credit repair service. The categorization as "credit-repair" is fundamentally incorrect; this company specializes in investment management, estate planning, and executive financial advisory. It is not appropriate for consumers seeking to improve credit scores or dispute credit report errors.
services: Personal banking (through Bank of America partnership)
